# Review Checklist: AgentOS Chat Search

**Purpose**: Validate requirements quality and readiness for PR planning across chat UX, API contracts, identity/data ownership, agent behavior, and Railway deployment.
**Created**: 2026-05-18
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are the primary chat flow requirements complete from message entry through sourced agent answer? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001–FR-006]
- [ ] CHK002 Are persisted history requirements complete for restore, active-session context, deletion, and exclusion from future context? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009–FR-018]
- [ ] CHK003 Are New Chat, Stop, safe thinking/progress, and streaming requirements all defined as distinct behaviors? [Completeness, Spec §FR-019–FR-023]
- [ ] CHK004 Are deployment requirements complete for local Makefile commands, two Railway services, required variables, smoke checks, and no GitHub Actions? [Completeness, Spec §FR-024–FR-031]
- [ ] CHK005 Are all data entities needed by the requirements represented in the data model, including identity, session, message, run, source, and deployment configuration? [Completeness, Data Model §Entities]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is "current identity's active chat session history" defined clearly enough to distinguish active, inactive, deleted, and other-identity sessions? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-018]
- [ ] CHK007 Is "Auth0-compatible identity" clarified with the mocked-identity boundary and future Auth0 replacement expectation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012–FR-013, Plan §Constitution Check]
- [ ] CHK008 Are "safe thinking/progress status" requirements explicit about allowed status content and prohibited hidden reasoning content? [Clarity, Spec §FR-021–FR-022]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "minimum CPU and memory settings" defined with exception documentation when Railway rejects or lacks a minimum setting? [Clarity, Spec §FR-030–FR-031]
- [ ] CHK010 Are source display requirements clear about when sources are required and what source metadata must be available? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004–FR-005, Data Model §SearchResult]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK011 Do the spec, data model, and API contract consistently treat chat history as owner-filtered server-side persistence rather than browser-only state? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009–FR-018, Data Model §Privacy and Ownership Rules, Contracts §OpenAPI]
- [ ] CHK012 Are Stop behavior requirements consistent between user scenarios, functional requirements, UI contract states, and tasks? [Consistency, Spec §US2, Spec §FR-020, UI Contract §State Contract, Tasks §US2]
- [ ] CHK013 Are New Chat requirements consistent about creating a new session without deleting prior sessions? [Consistency, Spec §US1, Spec §FR-019, UI Contract §Interaction Rules]
- [ ] CHK014 Are deployment requirements consistent across the spec, plan, quickstart, and tasks regarding local Makefile commands and no GitHub Actions? [Consistency, Spec §FR-024–FR-029, Plan §Technical Context, Quickstart §Railway Setup and Deploy, Tasks §US3]
- [ ] CHK015 Are pgvector and vector-search requirements consistently scoped so pgvector may be provisioned but vector search remains out of scope? [Consistency, Spec §Constitution Requirements, Plan §Technical Context, Research §PostgreSQL]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK016 Are success criteria measurable for response visibility, stream start, history restore, identity isolation, stopped responses, and deployment timing? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001–SC-014]
- [ ] CHK017 Are success criteria aligned with the user stories they validate, including chat success, failure recovery, and deployment publishing? [Traceability, Spec §User Scenarios, Spec §SC-001–SC-014]
- [ ] CHK018 Are deployment success criteria objective enough to evaluate resource sizing exceptions and smoke-check expectations? [Measurability, Spec §SC-012–SC-014]
- [ ] CHK019 Are security and ownership success criteria stated with exact inclusion/exclusion expectations rather than vague privacy language? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005–SC-008]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK020 Are primary, alternate, and recovery flows documented for ask-answer, New Chat, Stop, active-session restore, and Delete active session? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2]
- [ ] CHK021 Are exception scenarios documented for search failure, timeout, malformed agent output, invalid mocked identity, interrupted streams, and unavailable history? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK022 Are deployment partial-failure scenarios covered for missing secrets, missing Makefile targets, one-service deployment failure, and Railway resource setting rejection? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK023 Are requirements defined for accessibility-relevant chat states such as streaming updates, errors, Stop, and New Chat controls? [Coverage, UI Contract §Accessibility Requirements]
- [ ] CHK024 Are requirements defined for source visibility when search results are used and unsupported-answer fallback when grounding is insufficient? [Coverage, Spec §FR-004–FR-006]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK025 Are requirements explicit about what happens when active session history cannot be loaded before an agent run starts? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK026 Are requirements explicit about history deletion during an in-progress response? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK027 Are requirements explicit about repeated message submission while a prior response is still active? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK028 Are requirements explicit about streamed response interruption before completion? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK029 Are requirements explicit about Railway minimum resource settings being unavailable for a required service? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-031]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK030 Are performance requirements quantified for answer visibility, stream start, and history restore? [Non-Functional, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-004, Spec §SC-009]
- [ ] CHK031 Are latency validation tasks or manual measurement steps documented for SC-001, SC-004, and SC-009? [Coverage, Tasks §T076–T079, Quickstart]
- [ ] CHK032 Are observability requirements specific about which events need structured logs and trace fields? [Non-Functional, Plan §Constitution Check, Tasks §T040/T053]
- [ ] CHK033 Are security requirements specific about owner-filtered reads/writes, deleted-session exclusion, and no cross-identity context leakage? [Security, Spec §FR-012–FR-018]
- [ ] CHK034 Are deployment cost constraints stated clearly through minimum Railway CPU and memory settings? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-030–FR-031]
- [ ] CHK035 Are accessibility requirements defined for keyboard reachability, error messaging, transcript updates, and reduced-motion streaming readability? [Accessibility, UI Contract §Accessibility Requirements]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK036 Are external dependencies documented with failure expectations, including DuckDuckGo, Railway, PostgreSQL, model provider secrets, and mocked Auth0 identity? [Dependency, Spec §Edge Cases, Quickstart §Prerequisites]
- [ ] CHK037 Are assumptions about deferred Auth0, single-agent scope, no GitHub Actions, and DuckDuckGo-only search documented consistently? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK038 Are required environment variables and deployment variables specified with enough detail to support planning and task generation? [Dependency, Spec §FR-026, Quickstart §Local Environment]
- [ ] CHK039 Are limitations around no vector search and no multi-agent routing explicitly documented to prevent scope drift? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Technical Context]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK040 Is the boundary between safe progress status and prohibited hidden reasoning unambiguous for both backend and frontend requirements? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-021–FR-022]
- [ ] CHK041 Is the lifecycle of active versus inactive chat sessions clear enough to avoid conflicting interpretations during New Chat and context assembly? [Ambiguity, Data Model §ChatSession]
- [ ] CHK042 Are Makefile target expectations specific enough to avoid ambiguity between local run, deploy, status, logs, and smoke-check responsibilities? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-024, Quickstart §Railway Setup and Deploy]
- [ ] CHK043 Are documented Railway CPU/memory exceptions constrained enough to prevent silent deviation from minimum-resource requirements? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-031, Spec §SC-014]
- [ ] CHK044 Is mock-only identity explicitly constrained for non-production deployment until real Auth0 is integrated? [Consistency, Spec §Deployment Constraints, Plan §Constitution Check]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
